currency.source: Bank of Zambia (boz.zm), cross-rates from Wise/XE/ExchangeRates
currency.baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↓ ZMW = Kwacha strengthening (appreciating).
currency.mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. Next review: May 13, 2026.

ZAR.note: 1 ZMW = 0.942 ZAR. South African rand cross rate.

CNY.note: Chinese yuan. Zambia's growing yuan-denominated mining tax corridor.

drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
trendAnalysis.outlook: BOZ likely to continue easing MPR if inflation remains below 8%. Further kwacha appreciation possible in near term on copper tailwinds, but BOZ may intervene to protect export competitiveness. Key risks: global copper price reversal, drought cycles, election-related fiscal expansion (Aug 2026).
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.00 range. Bias toward further appreciation if copper holds above $9,500/tonne and IMF successor programme progresses.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Wider range reflects election uncertainty (Aug 2026). Post-election policy continuity would support kwacha; fiscal slippage would reverse gains.

marketClose.sessionSummary: 1,025 trades across 355,789 shares. Turnover K11.16 million. LASI flat at 25,689.97. ZFCO sole gainer (+K0.02). CHIL, DCZM, NATB declined. Trading in 22 of 28 listed securities.

analysis.marketConditionDetail: The LASI closed flat at 25,689.97 as only 4 stocks recorded price changes. Market cap including Shoprite Holdings reached K333.6 billion (K143.5 billion excluding Shoprite). Activity concentrated in ATEL (73% of value traded) and CECZ.

observations.title: ATEL - Liquidity Anchor Dominance
observations.detail: ATEL accounted for 73% of total value traded (ZMW 8.12M on 47,815 shares). At K169.87 with PE 10.02x and 7.06% dividend yield, it remains LuSE's most liquid counter. Concentration risk cuts both ways.

observations.title: CECZ - Highest Volume Counter
observations.detail: 98,323 shares traded (highest volume on the board). At K16.87 with PE 9.72x, CECZ offers copper-linked upside supported by the Green Bond solar expansion and DRC market growth.

observations.title: DCZM - Still Trading on Sentiment
observations.detail: At K24.60 with a 3.6% gross margin business, DCZM trades on the technology listing narrative rather than fundamentals. The eFuel and Smart Cards segments generated near-zero revenue in FY2025.

observations.title: ZSUG - K69.09, Thin Volume
observations.detail: Zambia Sugar closed at K69.09 on just 635 shares. With 75% held by ABF, the effective free float remains negligible. Great company, terrible trading stock.

observations.title: ZNCO - Deep Value Banking
observations.detail: At K9.57 with PE 6.87x and 4.58% dividend yield (35% payout ratio), Zanaco offers the best risk-adjusted value among LuSE financials. 7 consecutive years of dividend growth.

riskFlags: K190B of K333.6B market cap is Shoprite Holdings - only K143.5B in domestic Zambian equities
3 stocks recorded ZERO shares traded (INDO, PRIM, ENRG)
Government bond yields compete aggressively with equity returns
Single-stock concentration risk: ATEL = 73% of daily value traded
analysis.authorNote: Based on official LuSE daily stock data for June 5, 2026. This analysis is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice.

news.title: LASI Closes Flat at 25,689.97 — Low Activity Session
news.summary: The Lusaka All Share Index closed unchanged. 1,025 trades across the board with ATEL dominating value traded at ZMW 8.12M. 8 listed stocks recorded zero shares traded.

news.title: BoZ Holds Policy Rate at 13.5% Ahead of August Elections
news.summary: The Bank of Zambia maintained its policy rate at 13.5%, citing stabilizing inflation at 7.5% but cautioning on global energy price risks from the Iran conflict.

news.title: Zambia Posts Record Bumper Harvest — Maize Output Exceeds 4M Tonnes
news.summary: The Ministry of Agriculture confirmed a record maize harvest exceeding 4 million metric tonnes for the 2025/2026 season, driven by favorable rains and expanded FISP coverage.

news.title: Klapton Reinsurance IPO Oversubscribed — Lists March 2026
news.summary: Klapton Reinsurance confirmed its IPO was oversubscribed. The company, with K2.97B in gross written premiums and a Caa1 Moody's rating, is expected to list on LuSE Main Board this month.

news.title: Copper Prices Hold Above $10,000/tonne on AI Data Center Demand
news.summary: Copper prices continue to trade above $10,000/tonne, supported by AI data center buildout and EV manufacturing demand. Zambia's 2025 output hit a record 890,346 metric tonnes.

news.title: IMF Successor Programme Talks Progress — Staff Visit Scheduled July
news.summary: The IMF confirmed a staff visit to Lusaka in July 2026 to begin formal negotiations on a successor programme to the expired ECF. Government emphasizes 'growth-focused agenda.'

news.title: Zambia Sugar Crosses $1B Market Cap — But Daily Volume Remains Thin
news.summary: Zambia Sugar maintained its $1B+ valuation but continues to see extremely low trading volumes — just K89,926 traded on a recent session. 75% held by ABF.

news.title: DotCom Zambia Appoints New CTO — Signals SaaS Pivot
news.summary: DCZM announced the appointment of a new Chief Technology Officer with experience in fintech SaaS platforms, signaling a potential strategic pivot beyond toll processing.

news.title: Afreximbank Prices First Panda Bond After Fitch Exit
news.summary: Afreximbank successfully priced its inaugural Panda Bond on the Chinese interbank market, leveraging its AAA rating from China Chengxin (CCXI), marking a milestone in its pivot to Asian debt markets.

news.title: ZCCM-IH Announces Strategic Review of Mopani Copper Mines Stake
news.summary: ZCCM Investments Holdings confirmed it is undertaking a strategic review of its stake in Mopani Copper Mines. Potential outcomes include partial divestment or new strategic partnership.

news.title: CEC Renewables Secures $96.7M Green Bond for Solar Expansion
news.summary: CEC Renewables, a CECZ subsidiary, closed a $96.7M green bond issuance to fund solar-hybrid power projects for mining operations, targeting 200MW of new capacity by 2028.

news.title: Zambia's Yuan Mining Tax Channel — 6 Month Update Shows Growing Adoption
news.summary: The Bank of Zambia reported that yuan-denominated mining tax remittances have grown steadily since the October 2025 launch, with an increasing number of Chinese-operated mines utilizing the RMB corridor.

news.title: Airtel Zambia Reports 12% Revenue Growth — Mobile Money Drives Expansion
news.summary: Airtel Zambia reported 12% YoY revenue growth to ZMW 8.9B, driven by mobile money and data services. The company maintained its position as LuSE's most liquid counter.

news.title: Zambia's Debt-to-GDP Ratio Falls Below 70% — Finance Ministry
news.summary: The Ministry of Finance announced that Zambia's debt-to-GDP ratio has fallen below 70% following the successful restructuring, down from a peak of over 120% during the default period.

news.title: CECZ Wins Tender for 150MW Solar Plant at Lumwana Mine
news.summary: Copperbelt Energy Corporation won a competitive tender to build and operate a 150MW solar-hybrid plant for Barrick's Lumwana mine expansion, expected online by 2028.
